mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity valuations higher within one week as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies forward earnings expectations and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 55 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or correlation between HOUST and RSAFS breaks down during stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of capacity utilization recovery, industrial production growth directly lifts corporate profit margins by lowering unit labor costs, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 2 percent annually, indicating supply gluts rather than demand-driven margin expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, which immediately lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or GDP beats do not correspond to positive 5-day equity returns in more than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces anticipated monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month window.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL breaching the 2.5 percent threshold, or the signal fails to predict downturns during supply-side inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stagnant unemployment rate indicates structural labor market imbalances that trigger wage-price spirals, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k while UNRATE remains flat
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive equity returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the relationship reverses during recessionary payrolls contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1.0 million units annually during a monetary easing phase signals strong credit availability and consumer confidence, driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative when HOUST breaks the threshold during high-interest-rate environments, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a 10-year backtest.
